response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced full-stack engineer with genuine strengths in distributed systems, software craftsmanship, and mentoring. However, this role is built around deep C++ expertise (8 years required) and ownership of large-scale cloud compute infrastructure, and the resume shows only roughly six months of professional C++ work (National Instruments) plus a thesis project. The core technical stack here is TypeScript/Node/DLang web and product engineering, not systems-level C++ or production cloud platform architecture, so the fit against the minimum qualifications is weak despite the strong general profile.
strengths: High-throughput distributed systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and active mentoring/teaching
Broad full-stack and infrastructure exposure including Docker, Kubernetes, and DevOps tooling
concerns: Minimal C++ experience (~6 months) versus the required 8 years
No demonstrated production cloud compute platform or large-scale cloud architecture ownership
Career focus is web/product engineering rather than systems-level infrastructure
